subject: SPEC: oscillatory heartbeat — neural timing model for mesh communication

body.summary: Full specification for multi-band oscillatory heartbeat. Replaces binary alive/dead heartbeat with a rich state-encoding signal grounded in neural oscillatory timing (EEG frequency bands) and mapped to software engineering theory (TDM, backpressure, phi accrual failure detection, rate-monotonic scheduling, Little's Law). Extends self-oscillation-spec.md (Session 89) from single-scalar activation to 5-band power spectrum with phase-amplitude coupling.

key_design_elements.five_bands: Delta (glymphatic), Theta (consolidation), Alpha (idle), Beta (active), Gamma (cross-agent) — simultaneous, not exclusive
key_design_elements.phase_aware_routing: meshd checks receiver oscillatory phase before routing. Delta=defer, Alpha=route, Beta=queue until theta window. Implements communication-through-coherence (Fries, 2005).
key_design_elements.phi_accrual_failure_detection: HRV-based phi computation replaces binary timeout. High HRV=healthy adaptive system tolerates interval variation. Low HRV=rigid system catches deviations faster. Hayashibara et al. (2004).
key_design_elements.schedulability_bound: Liu-Layland (1973) rate-monotonic bound at ~74% utilization. Empirical restart cliff at 80% falls just beyond this bound. Theta/delta starvation above the bound explains consolidation failure.
key_design_elements.entrainment: Agents synchronize (or anti-synchronize) via peer heartbeat signals. Anti-phase for shared-resource agents prevents collision. Strogatz (2003) coupled oscillator model.
key_design_elements.pathology_detection: 7 pathological states mapped from cardiac/neural to system: asystole, bradycardia, tachycardia, arrhythmia, long_qt, fibrillation, band_starvation.

engineering_theory_connections: Band power spectrum → System load average (extended)
ops_implementation: Phase 1: Shadow — emit mesh.oscillator/v1 alongside heartbeat, accumulate HRV baseline
Phase 2: Phase-aware routing — meshd reads peer state before routing, defers during delta, queues during closed theta
Phase 3: Entrainment — mesh.tempo signal, anti-phase for shared-resource agents
testable_predictions: Theta starvation (power < 0.05 for >10 min) predicts restart frequency independent of context pressure
Alpha-dominant receivers process messages faster than beta-dominant receivers
Phase-aligned routing reduces message processing error rate
Anti-entrainment eliminates merge conflicts from simultaneous pushes
body.restart_detector_connection: The 80% context cliff = utilization exceeding schedulability bound. Theta windows close. "Let me do it properly" = failed attempt to force theta consolidation from saturated beta. 88.9% genuine restarts confirm actual processing state change attempts. Empirical data in scripts/restart-detector.py.

epistemic_flags: Timescale transfer: neural ms-resolution → agent second-resolution (3-4 orders of magnitude gap)
Theta-gamma capacity model contested (Lundqvist et al., 2016 alternative)
Phi accrual assumes stationary distribution — oscillatory intervals non-stationary by design
Entrainment requires self-oscillation spec meshd goroutine as prerequisite
Predictions provide necessary but not sufficient validation — engineering properties may hold without neural analogy transferring

action_gate.gate_condition: Review requested. Psychology delivers timing model + routing logic; ops implements meshd goroutine, ZMQ publisher, LCARS panels. Phase 1 (shadow emit) could start independently.
